positive.label: Scandi Blonde — image + text

input.client_notes: I want to be super bright blonde all over, like an icy Scandi blonde. I hate seeing any yellow or gold.

expected_output.description: Clean, icy, level 10 platinum blonde. Cool, reflective finish with violet to neutralise yellow. No warmth.

positive.why: Client says 'all over' = global, 'icy' + 'no yellow' = violet/pearl neutralisation needed. Level 10 is the only level that matches 'super bright blonde'.

positive.label: Brassy Balayage Correction — text only

input.client_notes: My balayage has gone super brassy and orange from the sun and washing. I want it cooler, creamy, and expensive-looking, but I don't want to lose my natural root.

expected_output.description: Creamy, cool-toned level 8-9 lived-in blonde. Neutralise orange and gold brassiness while preserving natural root shadow.

positive.why: Client wants to keep existing root and correct brassiness — this is toning, not lifting. 'Creamy' and 'cool' point to smoky beige/ash. 'Expensive-looking' = even, neutralised tone.

positive.label: Grey Blending Highlift — text only

input.client_notes: I need my roots done. I want to stay really bright blonde, cover these greys coming through, but I don't want bleach on my scalp. Keep it as cool as possible.

expected_output.description: Clean, bright, neutral-cool blonde at the root area, blending grey coverage seamlessly with existing level 10 ends. No bleach on scalp.

positive.why: 'No bleach on scalp' + grey coverage + bright blonde = highlift is the technique. 'Cool as possible' = ash. Grey blending requires a natural base for opacity, so natural ash is correct.

negative.label: Confusing balayage correction with balayage

input.client_notes: My balayage has gone super brassy and orange. I want it cooler and creamy.

bad_output.description: Fresh balayage with beige blonde tones.

negative.correction: Technique is wrong: client already has a balayage that needs correcting, not a new balayage. Correct technique is 'glaze/toning only'. Tone is also wrong: 'beige/gold' still contains warmth — client explicitly wants cooler. Should be 'smoky beige/ash'.

negative.label: Wrong level for icy platinum

input.client_notes: I want to be super bright blonde all over, like an icy Scandi blonde.

negative.correction: Level 8 is too low for 'icy Scandi blonde' — that is a level 10 result. At level 8, underlying pigment is yellow and the result would look warm, not icy. Tone should be 'violet/pearl' not just 'ash' — violet is needed specifically to neutralise the pale yellow at level 10.

negative.label: Suggesting lightening when client wants toning only

input.client_notes: My hair has gone brassy, I just want it toned back to cool blonde. Keep my natural root.

bad_output.description: Lift to level 10 and tone with pearl.

negative.correction: Client wants to preserve their root and correct brassiness — this is a toning-only job, not a lift. 'Global lightening' would blow out the natural root the client wants to keep. Correct technique is 'glaze/toning only'.
